toolUseResult.originalFile:
# Example run

A real, unedited run of the agent on:

> *Compare the carbon-capture approaches of Climeworks, Charm Industrial, and
> Heirloom. For each, summarise the core method and one recent milestone.*

Files in `carbon-capture/`:

- **`report.md`** — the final cited report the agent produced.
- **`trace.jsonl`** — every event the agent emitted (the same stream the web UI
  renders and the eval harness reads). Skim it to see the full plan→search→
  fetch→record→finish flow.
- **`state.json`** — the final structured state (plan, findings, sources).

What this run demonstrates (visible in the trace):

- An initial plan, then a **`plan_revised`** event — the agent re-planned mid-run.
- Multiple search/fetch cycles with 5 grounded findings across 3 cited sources.
- A **`nudge`** event — stall detection fired when progress stalled.
- A **`force_synthesis`** event — the agent hit its step budget, so the report
  was synthesised directly from the recorded (grounded) findings.

To watch a run like this live: `python -m uvicorn server.app:app` then open
http://localhost:8000.

-- A **`nudge`** event — stall detection fired when progress stalled.
+- Multiple search/fetch cycles with grounded findings across 3 cited sources.

was synthesised directly from the recorded (grounded) findings.

content.content: Updated task #10 status

content.text:
## Done — deepagents-inspired architecture upgrade shipped

I studied [langchain-ai/deepagents](https://github.com/langchain-ai/deepagents), mapped its **four pillars** against our agent, and — per your "keep it simple" choice — ported the two lighter pillars into our own framework-free loop while deliberately skipping sub-agents (the bigger win, noted as future work).

### What changed
| Pillar | What I added |
|---|---|
| **Filesystem memory** | `write_note`/`read_note` — a virtual scratchpad in state (persisted to `runs/<id>/notes/`). Only note **names + sizes** enter the context; bodies are pulled back on demand. The agent can now offload outlines/summaries so working memory exceeds what the prompt holds — and it's *scratch*, not evidence (grounded claims still go through `record_finding`). |
| **Planning/efficiency** | A targeted **"search-without-fetch" nudge** that fires when the agent queries repeatedly without reading a source — directly fixing the over-search weakness the eval had surfaced — plus a tighter search→fetch→record cadence in the system prompt. |

I avoided the trap of changing things without re-validating: tools/prompt changes invalidate the cassettes, so I **re-recorded the full eval** and re-verified offline replay.

### Measured impact (same `gpt-4.1-mini`, same task set)
- **Mean judge score 4.79 → 5.0**; deterministic checks **30/31**.
- Scratchpad **used on 4/6 tasks**; the cadence nudge tightened fetch ratios (the fault task now fetches every result it searches).
- **Offline replay reproduces the new scorecard exactly** (write-once cassettes held up with the new tools).
- New offline tests for notes + the nudge — **6/6 pass**.

### Honest remaining gap
`rust-vs-go` still fails `cited>=3`: it now *fetches* 5 sources but the final report cites only 2. The right fix (written up in the README) is a "distinct-sources-**cited** < target → keep going" gate before `finish`, rather than counting findings.

### Housekeeping
A stray `implement.md` (a captured dump of my earlier summary) had crept into the working tree — I left the file alone but added it to `.gitignore` so it stays out of the repo.

All four commits are on `github.com/mrzaid/research-agent` (latest: `b428efd`). The README's architecture section now documents the scratchpad and credits deepagents as the inspiration.

Sources: [langchain-ai/deepagents](https://github.com/langchain-ai/deepagents) · [Deep Agents overview — LangChain docs](https://docs.langchain.com/oss/python/deepagents/overview) · [DeepAgents Architecture: Planning, Sub-agents, File System](https://eastondev.com/blog/en/posts/ai/deepagents-architecture/)

content.content:
1	# deepagent — Final Implementation Plan (Take-Home Winning Build)
2	
3	> **For Claude Code.** This is the single source of truth. It merges two earlier drafts (v2 = improvements to the existing repo; v3 = an ambitious greenfield design) into one plan, filtered through the actual take-home brief and its grading weights. Read it fully before writing code. Build on the **existing `deepagent` repo** — do not rewrite it.
4	
5	---
6	
7	## 0. The strategy in one paragraph
8	
9	The brief explicitly rewards **the simplest harness that does the job well** and warns that **"overly/unjustifiably complex" solutions "count against you,"** in a **4–6 hour** box, graded **40% harness engineering, 30% evaluation, 15% prompt/context, 15% code quality & judgment.** The existing `deepagent` already is a narrow, single-threaded, well-understood agent with grounding, budgets, recovery, tracing, and an eval harness — i.e. it already fits the brief. So we **do not** rebuild it event-sourced or split it into multiple agents. We add a small number of high-signal upgrades that map directly onto the 40% and 30% buckets, we make the observability and the eval *undeniable in a demo*, and we **state in the README what we deliberately left out and why** (that judgment is 15% of the grade and is what makes a submission look senior).
10	
11	**What wins this: a fresh-context critic, a tighter recovery/observability story, and an eval harness you actually learned something from — delivered complete and polished. Not breadth.**
12	
13	---
14	
15	## 1. Foundation: what already exists (keep it)
16	
17	The current repo (`agent/`, `server/`, `eval/`, `runs/`) already implements, and we keep:
18	
19	- **Single-threaded plan→act→observe→adapt loop** with one tool call per step (`agent/loop.py`).
20	- **Reconstructed, bounded context** rebuilt from a structured `ResearchState` each turn (`agent/context.py`, `agent/state.py`) — not a growing transcript.
21	- **Enforced grounding at two gates:** `record_finding` rejects un-fetched URLs; `finish` rejects reports citing un-fetched URLs (`agent/tools/findings.py`, `finish.py`).
22	- **Budgets + nudges:** step + token budgets, stall detection, search-without-fetch nudge (`agent/loop.py`, `config.py`).
23	- **Graceful degradation:** `_force_synthesis` always produces a grounded best-effort report.
24	- **Unified observability:** structured `trace.jsonl` + live SSE web view (`agent/tracer.py`, `server/`).
25	- **Eval harness:** deterministic checks + LLM-judge + fault injection, deterministic offline via the cassette record/replay layers (`eval/`, `agent/llm.py`, `agent/cache.py`).
26	
27	This document only adds to that. The invariants below protect it.
28	
29	### Invariants — do NOT break
30	
31	1. **Single-threaded, one tool call per step.** No parallel/sub agents in the core (one optional read-only helper is a stretch only — §6).
32	2. **Two-gate enforced grounding** stays. A claim can only cite a fetched URL; a report can only cite fetched URLs.
33	3. **Reconstructed (not accumulated) context.** Everything the model sees is rebuilt from state each turn.
34	4. **Determinism under replay.** The eval suite runs `LLM_MODE=replay` offline. Every new LLM call must (a) go through `llm.chat`/`llm.simple` so it is cassette-keyed, and (b) have a deterministic non-LLM fallback so tests pass with no cassette. (See §5.)
35	5. **Crash-safe resume.** State persists after every step; any new state field must round-trip through `to_dict`/`from_dict`.
36	6. **Failures are data.** Tool errors become observations; never crash the run.
37	
38	---
39	
40	## 2. Scope decisions (the judgment section — mirror this in the README)
41	
42	### IN — because it maps to the grade and stays simple
43	
44	| Item | Source | Grade bucket | Why it's worth it |
45	|---|---|---|---|
46	| Fresh-context critic / verifier | v3 §8.2 + v2 WI-1 | Harness 40% / Eval 30% | Catches false success & hallucinated citations; single highest-signal addition; still one extra LLM call |
47	| Real context compaction | v2 WI-2 / v3 §7.2 | Context 15% / Harness | Makes the "bounded context" claim actually true as findings grow |
48	| Wall-clock budget + retry jitter | v2 WI-3/WI-4 / v3 §8.3/8.5 | Harness 40% | Completes the circuit-breaker story; near-zero code |
49	| Trajectory eval metrics + 3 injected-failure scenarios | v2 WI-5 / v3 §13 | Eval 30% | The 30% bucket; "what we learned" comes from here |
50	| Resume "get your bearings" turn | v3 §7.3 | Harness 40% | Strong anti-amnesia signal; a few lines on top of existing resume |
51	| CLI `trace` renderer + on-disk watchability | v3 §10 | Harness 40% (tracing is "a big plus") | Cheap, makes the demo/video vivid |
52	| Kill switch (`AGENT_STOP`) + optional steering | v3 §9 | Harness 40% | ~20 lines each; great recovery-story moment on video |
53	| README failure-mode scorecard + time/tradeoffs + legal extension | v3 §15 + brief | Judgment 15% | Turns the README into the argument for hiring you |
54	
55	### OUT — and say why in the README (this restraint is the point)
56	
57	- **Event-sourcing rewrite.** `state.json` (a fold persisted each step) already gives correct resume. Re-architecting to an append-only event log is complexity the brief penalizes.
58	- **Separate planner/worker/critic *agents* / services.** Keep planning as the existing `update_plan` tool inside the one loop; the critic is one extra stateless call, not a third service.
59	- **git-per-run, sqlite-vec/Chroma store, browser-verified sourcing, rubric critic, sub-agent fan-out, web-UI rebuild.** All stretch-only or cut. Mention you considered them and chose not to.
60	- **Switching provider SDK.** Keep the existing stack; provider choice is irrelevant to the grade.
61	
62	> README line to use: *"I deliberately kept this a single-threaded loop with a JSON state file rather than an event-sourced, multi-agent system. The brief rewards the simplest harness that works; the extra machinery would have been unjustifiable complexity and risked a half-finished submission. Here's the one place I'd add a read-only sub-agent, and why it's off by default…"*
63	
64	---
65	
66	## 3. Work items (build in this order; commit each with tests green)
67	
68	### WI-1 (P0) — Fresh-context critic / verifier
69	**Why.** Today `finish` only checks citations were *fetched*, not that the source *supports* the claim. Add an independent critic (Reflexion + Anthropic fresh-context evaluator) that grades the draft before the run may complete.
70	
71	- **New `agent/verifier.py`:** `verify(state) -> list[dict]`. One batched `llm.simple` call (`model=config.SUMMARY_MODEL`) given **only** the goal, the findings ledger (claim + quote + source summary), and the draft report — **not** the worker's reasoning/recent actions. Returns issues: uncited claims, citations whose source text doesn't support the claim, unmet goal coverage. Parse JSON defensively (reuse `eval/judge.py` approach).
72	- **Deterministic fallback:** with no LLM/cassette, flag a finding only if its `quote` is empty. Keeps offline tests meaningful.
73	- **Loop integration (`agent/loop.py`):** when `finish` succeeds, run `verify`. If issues remain and `state.verify_retries < config.MAX_VERIFY_RETRIES`: set `done=False`, increment counter, set the next-step `note` to the critique, emit `verify_failed`, `continue`. On exhaustion, accept and emit `verify_exhausted`. Also run once (no retry) inside `_force_synthesis`.
74	- **Prompt:** add `CRITIC` to `agent/prompts.py` — "cold, independent reviewer who did not see how this was built."
75	- **Config:** `VERIFY_ENABLED=_bool(...,True)`, `MAX_VERIFY_RETRIES=_int(...,2)` (add a `_bool` helper).
76	- **State:** `verify_retries:int=0` (round-trip it).
77	- **Tracer:** `verify_started`, `verify_failed`, `verify_exhausted`, `verify_result`.
78	- **Accept:** offline tests (empty-quote flagged, good quote passes; perpetual-fail run still terminates). New eval check `claims_verified`.
79	
80	### WI-2 (P0) — Real context compaction
81	**Why.** `_render_findings`/`_render_sources` render *every* item each turn — the prompt grows linearly. Make it actually bounded (Anthropic context-engineering).
82	
83	- Render the most recent `COMPACT_FINDINGS_RECENT` findings verbatim; collapse older ones into `state.findings_digest`. Same for sources via `state.sources_digest`. **Never drop findings/sources from state** — `finish`/`_force_synthesis` still see all of them. Keep stable `[n]` source numbering.
84	- Compaction routine (cheap model, every `COMPACT_EVERY` steps once over `COMPACT_*_AFTER`); deterministic fallback = truncate/concat older claim texts.
85	- **Config:** `COMPACT_FINDINGS_AFTER=12`, `COMPACT_FINDINGS_RECENT=6`, `COMPACT_SOURCES_AFTER=15`, `COMPACT_EVERY=4`.
86	- **State:** `findings_digest=""`, `sources_digest=""` (round-trip).
87	- **Tracer:** `compaction`.
88	- **Accept:** test that a 40-finding state renders below a fixed length and stays flat as more are added; `finish` still sees all findings.
89	
90	### WI-3 (P0) — Wall-clock budget
91	- `agent/loop.py`: `deadline = time.monotonic() + config.WALL_CLOCK_BUDGET` (if > 0) at start; add to the `while` condition; on breach emit `budget_event{kind:"wall_clock"}` and fall to `_force_synthesis`. Recompute on resume (don't persist); comment it.
92	- **Config:** `WALL_CLOCK_BUDGET=_int(...,600)` (0 disables).
93	- **Accept:** tiny budget (monkeypatched) → exits via wall-clock, still produces a grounded report.
94	
95	### WI-4 (P0) — Retry backoff with jitter
96	- `agent/llm.py`: replace `time.sleep(min(2**attempt,8))` with `time.sleep(random.uniform(0, min(2**attempt,8)))`; `import random`.
97	- **Accept:** patched-sleep test asserts the arg stays in `[0,8]`.
98	
99	### WI-5 (P0) — Eval: trajectory metrics + injected-failure scenarios
100	**Why.** This is the 30% bucket and the source of "what we learned."
101	
102	- **Metrics (`eval/harness.py`, derived from the trace):** `tool_error_rate`, `replans`, `nudges` (by kind), `verify_retries`, `compactions`. Print a compact form in `_print_scorecard`; keep full set in results JSON.
103	- **Scenarios (`eval/tasks.py` + a small failure-injection toggle in `agent/tools/fetch.py`/`search.py`, recorded/mock-backed so offline & deterministic):** beyond the existing `fail_first_fetch`, add:
104	  1. **Empty/garbage results** for a sub-question → assert the criterion stays *unsupported* (no false success) and the report names the gap.
105	  2. **Contradictory sources** → assert the agent surfaces the conflict rather than silently picking one.
106	  3. **Hallucinated citation** (worker cites a source that doesn't support the claim) → assert the **critic (WI-1) catches it** and the loop corrects.
107	  4. **Kill + resume** → assert `ResearchState` reconstructs (same findings/criteria, no duplicate side effects) and the run finishes.
108	- **Accept:** `LLM_MODE=replay python -m eval run --no-judge` passes with the new fields present; the four scenarios assert their behaviors.
109	
110	### WI-6 (P1) — Resume "get your bearings" turn
111	- On `resume`, before any new tool call, run one reorientation prompt (`reorient`-style) that has the model read the current state (goal, plan, findings, what's outstanding) and state in ≤5 sentences where things stand and the single next criterion. Emit `reoriented`. Tiny code, strong anti-amnesia signal.
112	- **Accept:** resumed run emits `reoriented` and continues correctly.
113	
114	### WI-7 (P1) — CLI `trace` renderer + watchability
115	- `python -m agent trace <run_id> [--verbose]`: render `trace.jsonl` as a readable step→action→observation→budget timeline.
116	- README: document `tail -f runs/<id>/trace.jsonl`, `watch -n2 'python -m agent trace <id> | tail -n 30'`. (Tracing is explicitly "a big plus" in the 40% bucket.)
117	- **Accept:** renders a finished run without error.
118	
119	### WI-8 (P1) — Kill switch + steering (operator controls)
120	- Before each tool execution, check for `runs/<id>/AGENT_STOP` (and a SIGINT handler) → halt cleanly: persist, emit `run_ended{status:"aborted"}`, exit. `touch` that file to stop a runaway run.
121	- Before each worker turn, if `runs/<id>/STEER.md` exists, inject its contents as a one-time note, emit `steer_injected`, delete it.
122	- **Accept:** writing `AGENT_STOP` mid-run stops it cleanly and it can `resume`; `STEER.md` content appears in the next turn's context once.
123	
124	### WI-9 (P2, optional) — small polish
125	- `record_finding` dedup on `(claim, source_url)`; windowed loop-signature detector (ring buffer, `REPEAT_LIMIT`). Do only if time remains.
126	
127	---
128	
129	## 4. Prompts (own them, keep at the right altitude)
130	
131	Keep the existing `SYSTEM`/`SYNTHESIS` in `agent/prompts.py`. Add:
132	- **`CRITIC`** (WI-1): cold independent reviewer; PASS only if every reported claim is supported by a cited, fetched source; flag uncited claims, unsupported citations, coverage gaps; terse, with a fix hint each.
133	- **`REORIENT`** (WI-6): "You are resuming. From the state shown, in ≤5 sentences say where the work stands and the single next unsupported item, then proceed."
134	
135	---
136	
137	## 5. Cross-cutting rule for every new LLM call (don't break replay)
138	
139	For WI-1, WI-2, WI-6: (1) route through `llm.simple`/`llm.chat` so it's cassette-keyed; (2) provide a **deterministic non-LLM fallback** so `LLM_MODE=replay` tests pass without new cassettes; (3) use `config.SUMMARY_MODEL`; (4) add `state.tokens_used += resp.usage.get("total_tokens",0)`; (5) if you want them in the eval baseline, record cassettes with `LLM_MODE=record` and commit under `eval/cassettes/`.
140	
141	---
142	
143	## 6. The one allowed (off-by-default) sub-agent — stretch only
144	
145	If and only if everything above is done and polished: a **read-only research sub-agent** for a single broad sub-question that returns a ≤1–2k-token condensed summary into the ledger (Cognition's one endorsed use). Off by default behind a flag. Document the ~15× token tradeoff. **Do not** build this if it risks the core being unfinished.
146	
147	---
148	
149	## 7. Deliverables checklist (the brief lists six)
150	
151	1. **Source code** — public git repo, clear structure, run instructions in README.
152	2. **README** — in your own words: how it works, key decisions + *why*, the **OUT-of-scope/judgment** section (§2), how you spent the time and tradeoffs, what you'd do with more time, and a "how this extends to a legal/compliance / due-diligence workflow" paragraph. Include the **failure-mode scorecard** table below.
153	3. **Eval harness + results** — `python -m eval run`; paste the scorecard table and a short "what I learned / what I'd improve" (from WI-5).
154	4. **Example run** — one real task start→finish committed under `runs/` (or `examples/`), with its `trace.jsonl` and `report.md`.
155	5. **3–5 min video** — narrate: goal → plan → one loop step (think→tool→observe) → a failure being recovered (use the kill switch or an injected failure) → the critic catching something → final cited report + the `trace` view. The recovery + critic moments are the wow.
156	6. **`build_sessions/`** — export raw Claude Code session logs; do not edit them.
157	
158	### README failure-mode scorecard (fill in with real code/eval pointers)
159	
160	| Failure mode | How deepagent handles it | Code | Proven by |
161	|---|---|---|---|
162	| Runaway loop / no termination | step + token + wall-clock budgets, kill switch | `loop.py`, `config.py`, WI-3/WI-8 | eval: budget-breach, kill+resume |
163	| Context growth ("dumb zone") | bounded reconstructed window + compaction | `context.py`, WI-2 | length test |
164	| Amnesia on resume | `state.json` fold + reorientation turn | `state.py`, WI-6 | eval: kill+resume |
165	| False success / premature done | two-gate grounding + fresh-context critic | `findings.py`, `finish.py`, `verifier.py` | eval: hallucinated-citation |
166	| Hallucinated citations | critic verifies claim vs source text | `verifier.py` | eval: hallucinated-citation |
167	| Tool failures (timeout/429/garbage) | retries+jitter, actionable errors, graceful degrade | `llm.py`, `tools/*`, `loop.py` | eval: timeout/empty/contradictory |
168	| Stuck / repeating | stall + search-without-fetch nudges (+ windowed detector) | `loop.py`, WI-9 | eval: loop trap |
169	| Black box | structured trace + `trace` CLI + SSE UI | `tracer.py`, `server/`, WI-7 | `trace` output |
170	
171	---
172	
173	## 8. Time budget (≈4–6h, where the grade is)
174	
175	1. **WI-4 jitter** + **WI-3 wall-clock** — ~20 min (warm-up, guardrails complete).
176	2. **WI-1 critic** — ~90 min. *The biggest score mover (40% + 30%).*
177	3. **WI-5 eval scenarios + metrics** — ~90 min. *The 30% bucket; produces "what I learned."*
178	4. **WI-2 compaction** — ~45 min.
179	5. **WI-6 reorientation + WI-7 trace CLI + WI-8 kill/steer** — ~60 min (cheap, high demo value).
180	6. **README scorecard + example run + record the video** — remaining time. *The README and video are where judgment (15%) is sold.*
181	
182	If you run short: ship after step 3. Critic + eval + the existing harness already make a complete, brief-aligned submission. WI-2/6/7/8 are upside, not prerequisites. **A finished, narrow agent beats an ambitious half-built one — that is the explicit grading instruction.**
183	
184	---
185	
186	*End of plan. Build on the existing repo, keep the loop small, spend time on the critic, the eval, and the story.*
187

file.content:
# deepagent — Final Implementation Plan (Take-Home Winning Build)

> **For Claude Code.** This is the single source of truth. It merges two earlier drafts (v2 = improvements to the existing repo; v3 = an ambitious greenfield design) into one plan, filtered through the actual take-home brief and its grading weights. Read it fully before writing code. Build on the **existing `deepagent` repo** — do not rewrite it.

---

## 0. The strategy in one paragraph

The brief explicitly rewards **the simplest harness that does the job well** and warns that **"overly/unjustifiably complex" solutions "count against you,"** in a **4–6 hour** box, graded **40% harness engineering, 30% evaluation, 15% prompt/context, 15% code quality & judgment.** The existing `deepagent` already is a narrow, single-threaded, well-understood agent with grounding, budgets, recovery, tracing, and an eval harness — i.e. it already fits the brief. So we **do not** rebuild it event-sourced or split it into multiple agents. We add a small number of high-signal upgrades that map directly onto the 40% and 30% buckets, we make the observability and the eval *undeniable in a demo*, and we **state in the README what we deliberately left out and why** (that judgment is 15% of the grade and is what makes a submission look senior).

**What wins this: a fresh-context critic, a tighter recovery/observability story, and an eval harness you actually learned something from — delivered complete and polished. Not breadth.**

---

## 1. Foundation: what already exists (keep it)

The current repo (`agent/`, `server/`, `eval/`, `runs/`) already implements, and we keep:

- **Single-threaded plan→act→observe→adapt loop** with one tool call per step (`agent/loop.py`).
- **Reconstructed, bounded context** rebuilt from a structured `ResearchState` each turn (`agent/context.py`, `agent/state.py`) — not a growing transcript.
- **Enforced grounding at two gates:** `record_finding` rejects un-fetched URLs; `finish` rejects reports citing un-fetched URLs (`agent/tools/findings.py`, `finish.py`).
- **Budgets + nudges:** step + token budgets, stall detection, search-without-fetch nudge (`agent/loop.py`, `config.py`).
- **Graceful degradation:** `_force_synthesis` always produces a grounded best-effort report.
- **Unified observability:** structured `trace.jsonl` + live SSE web view (`agent/tracer.py`, `server/`).
- **Eval harness:** deterministic checks + LLM-judge + fault injection, deterministic offline via the cassette record/replay layers (`eval/`, `agent/llm.py`, `agent/cache.py`).

This document only adds to that. The invariants below protect it.

### Invariants — do NOT break

1. **Single-threaded, one tool call per step.** No parallel/sub agents in the core (one optional read-only helper is a stretch only — §6).
2. **Two-gate enforced grounding** stays. A claim can only cite a fetched URL; a report can only cite fetched URLs.
3. **Reconstructed (not accumulated) context.** Everything the model sees is rebuilt from state each turn.
4. **Determinism under replay.** The eval suite runs `LLM_MODE=replay` offline. Every new LLM call must (a) go through `llm.chat`/`llm.simple` so it is cassette-keyed, and (b) have a deterministic non-LLM fallback so tests pass with no cassette. (See §5.)
5. **Crash-safe resume.** State persists after every step; any new state field must round-trip through `to_dict`/`from_dict`.
6. **Failures are data.** Tool errors become observations; never crash the run.

---

## 2. Scope decisions (the judgment section — mirror this in the README)

### IN — because it maps to the grade and stays simple

| Item | Source | Grade bucket | Why it's worth it |
|---|---|---|---|
| Fresh-context critic / verifier | v3 §8.2 + v2 WI-1 | Harness 40% / Eval 30% | Catches false success & hallucinated citations; single highest-signal addition; still one extra LLM call |
| Real context compaction | v2 WI-2 / v3 §7.2 | Context 15% / Harness | Makes the "bounded context" claim actually true as findings grow |
| Wall-clock budget + retry jitter | v2 WI-3/WI-4 / v3 §8.3/8.5 | Harness 40% | Completes the circuit-breaker story; near-zero code |
| Trajectory eval metrics + 3 injected-failure scenarios | v2 WI-5 / v3 §13 | Eval 30% | The 30% bucket; "what we learned" comes from here |
| Resume "get your bearings" turn | v3 §7.3 | Harness 40% | Strong anti-amnesia signal; a few lines on top of existing resume |
| CLI `trace` renderer + on-disk watchability | v3 §10 | Harness 40% (tracing is "a big plus") | Cheap, makes the demo/video vivid |
| Kill switch (`AGENT_STOP`) + optional steering | v3 §9 | Harness 40% | ~20 lines each; great recovery-story moment on video |
| README failure-mode scorecard + time/tradeoffs + legal extension | v3 §15 + brief | Judgment 15% | Turns the README into the argument for hiring you |

### OUT — and say why in the README (this restraint is the point)

- **Event-sourcing rewrite.** `state.json` (a fold persisted each step) already gives correct resume. Re-architecting to an append-only event log is complexity the brief penalizes.
- **Separate planner/worker/critic *agents* / services.** Keep planning as the existing `update_plan` tool inside the one loop; the critic is one extra stateless call, not a third service.
- **git-per-run, sqlite-vec/Chroma store, browser-verified sourcing, rubric critic, sub-agent fan-out, web-UI rebuild.** All stretch-only or cut. Mention you considered them and chose not to.
- **Switching provider SDK.** Keep the existing stack; provider choice is irrelevant to the grade.

> README line to use: *"I deliberately kept this a single-threaded loop with a JSON state file rather than an event-sourced, multi-agent system. The brief rewards the simplest harness that works; the extra machinery would have been unjustifiable complexity and risked a half-finished submission. Here's the one place I'd add a read-only sub-agent, and why it's off by default…"*

---

## 3. Work items (build in this order; commit each with tests green)

### WI-1 (P0) — Fresh-context critic / verifier
**Why.** Today `finish` only checks citations were *fetched*, not that the source *supports* the claim. Add an independent critic (Reflexion + Anthropic fresh-context evaluator) that grades the draft before the run may complete.

- **New `agent/verifier.py`:** `verify(state) -> list[dict]`. One batched `llm.simple` call (`model=config.SUMMARY_MODEL`) given **only** the goal, the findings ledger (claim + quote + source summary), and the draft report — **not** the worker's reasoning/recent actions. Returns issues: uncited claims, citations whose source text doesn't support the claim, unmet goal coverage. Parse JSON defensively (reuse `eval/judge.py` approach).
- **Deterministic fallback:** with no LLM/cassette, flag a finding only if its `quote` is empty. Keeps offline tests meaningful.
- **Loop integration (`agent/loop.py`):** when `finish` succeeds, run `verify`. If issues remain and `state.verify_retries < config.MAX_VERIFY_RETRIES`: set `done=False`, increment counter, set the next-step `note` to the critique, emit `verify_failed`, `continue`. On exhaustion, accept and emit `verify_exhausted`. Also run once (no retry) inside `_force_synthesis`.
- **Prompt:** add `CRITIC` to `agent/prompts.py` — "cold, independent reviewer who did not see how this was built."
- **Config:** `VERIFY_ENABLED=_bool(...,True)`, `MAX_VERIFY_RETRIES=_int(...,2)` (add a `_bool` helper).
- **State:** `verify_retries:int=0` (round-trip it).
- **Tracer:** `verify_started`, `verify_failed`, `verify_exhausted`, `verify_result`.
- **Accept:** offline tests (empty-quote flagged, good quote passes; perpetual-fail run still terminates). New eval check `claims_verified`.

### WI-2 (P0) — Real context compaction
**Why.** `_render_findings`/`_render_sources` render *every* item each turn — the prompt grows linearly. Make it actually bounded (Anthropic context-engineering).

- Render the most recent `COMPACT_FINDINGS_RECENT` findings verbatim; collapse older ones into `state.findings_digest`. Same for sources via `state.sources_digest`. **Never drop findings/sources from state** — `finish`/`_force_synthesis` still see all of them. Keep stable `[n]` source numbering.
- Compaction routine (cheap model, every `COMPACT_EVERY` steps once over `COMPACT_*_AFTER`); deterministic fallback = truncate/concat older claim texts.
- **Config:** `COMPACT_FINDINGS_AFTER=12`, `COMPACT_FINDINGS_RECENT=6`, `COMPACT_SOURCES_AFTER=15`, `COMPACT_EVERY=4`.
- **State:** `findings_digest=""`, `sources_digest=""` (round-trip).
- **Tracer:** `compaction`.
- **Accept:** test that a 40-finding state renders below a fixed length and stays flat as more are added; `finish` still sees all findings.

### WI-3 (P0) — Wall-clock budget
- `agent/loop.py`: `deadline = time.monotonic() + config.WALL_CLOCK_BUDGET` (if > 0) at start; add to the `while` condition; on breach emit `budget_event{kind:"wall_clock"}` and fall to `_force_synthesis`. Recompute on resume (don't persist); comment it.
- **Config:** `WALL_CLOCK_BUDGET=_int(...,600)` (0 disables).
- **Accept:** tiny budget (monkeypatched) → exits via wall-clock, still produces a grounded report.

### WI-4 (P0) — Retry backoff with jitter
- `agent/llm.py`: replace `time.sleep(min(2**attempt,8))` with `time.sleep(random.uniform(0, min(2**attempt,8)))`; `import random`.
- **Accept:** patched-sleep test asserts the arg stays in `[0,8]`.

### WI-5 (P0) — Eval: trajectory metrics + injected-failure scenarios
**Why.** This is the 30% bucket and the source of "what we learned."

- **Metrics (`eval/harness.py`, derived from the trace):** `tool_error_rate`, `replans`, `nudges` (by kind), `verify_retries`, `compactions`. Print a compact form in `_print_scorecard`; keep full set in results JSON.
- **Scenarios (`eval/tasks.py` + a small failure-injection toggle in `agent/tools/fetch.py`/`search.py`, recorded/mock-backed so offline & deterministic):** beyond the existing `fail_first_fetch`, add:
  1. **Empty/garbage results** for a sub-question → assert the criterion stays *unsupported* (no false success) and the report names the gap.
  2. **Contradictory sources** → assert the agent surfaces the conflict rather than silently picking one.
  3. **Hallucinated citation** (worker cites a source that doesn't support the claim) → assert the **critic (WI-1) catches it** and the loop corrects.
  4. **Kill + resume** → assert `ResearchState` reconstructs (same findings/criteria, no duplicate side effects) and the run finishes.
- **Accept:** `LLM_MODE=replay python -m eval run --no-judge` passes with the new fields present; the four scenarios assert their behaviors.

### WI-6 (P1) — Resume "get your bearings" turn
- On `resume`, before any new tool call, run one reorientation prompt (`reorient`-style) that has the model read the current state (goal, plan, findings, what's outstanding) and state in ≤5 sentences where things stand and the single next criterion. Emit `reoriented`. Tiny code, strong anti-amnesia signal.
- **Accept:** resumed run emits `reoriented` and continues correctly.

### WI-7 (P1) — CLI `trace` renderer + watchability
- `python -m agent trace <run_id> [--verbose]`: render `trace.jsonl` as a readable step→action→observation→budget timeline.
- README: document `tail -f runs/<id>/trace.jsonl`, `watch -n2 'python -m agent trace <id> | tail -n 30'`. (Tracing is explicitly "a big plus" in the 40% bucket.)
- **Accept:** renders a finished run without error.

### WI-8 (P1) — Kill switch + steering (operator controls)
- Before each tool execution, check for `runs/<id>/AGENT_STOP` (and a SIGINT handler) → halt cleanly: persist, emit `run_ended{status:"aborted"}`, exit. `touch` that file to stop a runaway run.
- Before each worker turn, if `runs/<id>/STEER.md` exists, inject its contents as a one-time note, emit `steer_injected`, delete it.
- **Accept:** writing `AGENT_STOP` mid-run stops it cleanly and it can `resume`; `STEER.md` content appears in the next turn's context once.

### WI-9 (P2, optional) — small polish
- `record_finding` dedup on `(claim, source_url)`; windowed loop-signature detector (ring buffer, `REPEAT_LIMIT`). Do only if time remains.

---

## 4. Prompts (own them, keep at the right altitude)

Keep the existing `SYSTEM`/`SYNTHESIS` in `agent/prompts.py`. Add:
- **`CRITIC`** (WI-1): cold independent reviewer; PASS only if every reported claim is supported by a cited, fetched source; flag uncited claims, unsupported citations, coverage gaps; terse, with a fix hint each.
- **`REORIENT`** (WI-6): "You are resuming. From the state shown, in ≤5 sentences say where the work stands and the single next unsupported item, then proceed."

---

## 5. Cross-cutting rule for every new LLM call (don't break replay)

For WI-1, WI-2, WI-6: (1) route through `llm.simple`/`llm.chat` so it's cassette-keyed; (2) provide a **deterministic non-LLM fallback** so `LLM_MODE=replay` tests pass without new cassettes; (3) use `config.SUMMARY_MODEL`; (4) add `state.tokens_used += resp.usage.get("total_tokens",0)`; (5) if you want them in the eval baseline, record cassettes with `LLM_MODE=record` and commit under `eval/cassettes/`.

---

## 6. The one allowed (off-by-default) sub-agent — stretch only

If and only if everything above is done and polished: a **read-only research sub-agent** for a single broad sub-question that returns a ≤1–2k-token condensed summary into the ledger (Cognition's one endorsed use). Off by default behind a flag. Document the ~15× token tradeoff. **Do not** build this if it risks the core being unfinished.

---

## 7. Deliverables checklist (the brief lists six)

1. **Source code** — public git repo, clear structure, run instructions in README.
2. **README** — in your own words: how it works, key decisions + *why*, the **OUT-of-scope/judgment** section (§2), how you spent the time and tradeoffs, what you'd do with more time, and a "how this extends to a legal/compliance / due-diligence workflow" paragraph. Include the **failure-mode scorecard** table below.
3. **Eval harness + results** — `python -m eval run`; paste the scorecard table and a short "what I learned / what I'd improve" (from WI-5).
4. **Example run** — one real task start→finish committed under `runs/` (or `examples/`), with its `trace.jsonl` and `report.md`.
5. **3–5 min video** — narrate: goal → plan → one loop step (think→tool→observe) → a failure being recovered (use the kill switch or an injected failure) → the critic catching something → final cited report + the `trace` view. The recovery + critic moments are the wow.
6. **`build_sessions/`** — export raw Claude Code session logs; do not edit them.

### README failure-mode scorecard (fill in with real code/eval pointers)

| Failure mode | How deepagent handles it | Code | Proven by |
|---|---|---|---|
| Runaway loop / no termination | step + token + wall-clock budgets, kill switch | `loop.py`, `config.py`, WI-3/WI-8 | eval: budget-breach, kill+resume |
| Context growth ("dumb zone") | bounded reconstructed window + compaction | `context.py`, WI-2 | length test |
| Amnesia on resume | `state.json` fold + reorientation turn | `state.py`, WI-6 | eval: kill+resume |
| False success / premature done | two-gate grounding + fresh-context critic | `findings.py`, `finish.py`, `verifier.py` | eval: hallucinated-citation |
| Hallucinated citations | critic verifies claim vs source text | `verifier.py` | eval: hallucinated-citation |
| Tool failures (timeout/429/garbage) | retries+jitter, actionable errors, graceful degrade | `llm.py`, `tools/*`, `loop.py` | eval: timeout/empty/contradictory |
| Stuck / repeating | stall + search-without-fetch nudges (+ windowed detector) | `loop.py`, WI-9 | eval: loop trap |
| Black box | structured trace + `trace` CLI + SSE UI | `tracer.py`, `server/`, WI-7 | `trace` output |

---

## 8. Time budget (≈4–6h, where the grade is)

1. **WI-4 jitter** + **WI-3 wall-clock** — ~20 min (warm-up, guardrails complete).
2. **WI-1 critic** — ~90 min. *The biggest score mover (40% + 30%).*
3. **WI-5 eval scenarios + metrics** — ~90 min. *The 30% bucket; produces "what I learned."*
4. **WI-2 compaction** — ~45 min.
5. **WI-6 reorientation + WI-7 trace CLI + WI-8 kill/steer** — ~60 min (cheap, high demo value).
6. **README scorecard + example run + record the video** — remaining time. *The README and video are where judgment (15%) is sold.*

If you run short: ship after step 3. Critic + eval + the existing harness already make a complete, brief-aligned submission. WI-2/6/7/8 are upside, not prerequisites. **A finished, narrow agent beats an ambitious half-built one — that is the explicit grading instruction.**

---

*End of plan. Build on the existing repo, keep the loop small, spend time on the critic, the eval, and the story.*

"""The agent control loop: plan -> act -> observe -> adapt.

This is the core deliverable. A single explicit loop where, each turn, the model
sees the reconstructed state and freely chooses ONE next tool. Robustness is
built in: step/token budgets bound the run, tool failures become observations
the model can react to, stall detection nudges the model out of loops, and a
force-synthesis fallback guarantees a grounded report even if the budget runs
out before the model calls finish.
"""
from __future__ import annotations

import json
from dataclasses import asdict
from typing import Optional

from . import config, llm
from .context import build_context
from .prompts import SYNTHESIS
from .state import ResearchState, Turn
from .tools import TOOL_SCHEMAS, dispatch
from .tracer import Tracer


def run(
    goal: str,
    run_id: Optional[str] = None,
    fault: Optional[dict] = None,
    resume: bool = False,
    max_steps: Optional[int] = None,
) -> ResearchState:
    if resume and run_id:
        state = ResearchState.load(run_id)
        state.run_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
        state.pages_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    else:
        state = ResearchState.new(goal, run_id=run_id, fault=fault)

    tracer = Tracer(state.run_id, state.run_dir)
    step_budget = max_steps or config.STEP_BUDGET
    if state.steps == 0:
        tracer.emit("run_started", {"goal": goal, "run_id": state.run_id, "step_budget": step_budget})

    while not state.done and state.steps < step_budget and state.tokens_used < config.TOKEN_BUDGET:
        # ---- decide whether to nudge the model -------------------------
        # Two nudges, most-specific first: (1) searching repeatedly without ever
        # reading a source (the over-search failure mode the eval surfaced), and
        # (2) general stall (repeating actions / no new evidence).
        note = None
        if state.searches_since_fetch >= config.SEARCH_WITHOUT_FETCH_LIMIT:
            note = (f"You have run {state.searches_since_fetch} searches in a row without "
                    "reading a source. Stop searching and fetch_url the most promising "
                    "result now — snippets are not evidence.")
            state.searches_since_fetch = 0
            state.nudges += 1
            tracer.emit("nudge", {"step": state.steps + 1, "kind": "search_without_fetch"})
        elif state.stall_counter >= config.STALL_LIMIT:
            note = ("You appear to be stuck (repeating actions or not gathering new "
                    "evidence). Change approach: try a different query, fetch a "
                    "different source, or call finish if you have enough findings.")
            state.stall_counter = 0
            state.nudges += 1
            tracer.emit("nudge", {"step": state.steps + 1, "kind": "stall"})

        state.steps += 1
        tracer.emit("step_started", {"step": state.steps})
        messages = build_context(state, note=note, step_budget=step_budget)

        # ---- ask the model for the next action --------------------------
        try:
            resp = llm.chat(messages, tools=TOOL_SCHEMAS, tool_choice="required")
        except Exception as e:  # noqa: BLE001
            tracer.emit("error", {"where": "llm", "msg": str(e)})
            break
        state.tokens_used += resp.usage.get("total_tokens", 0)

        if not resp.tool_calls:
            state.stall_counter += 1
            tracer.emit("observation", {"step": state.steps, "tool": None, "ok": False,
                                        "observation": "model returned no tool call"})
            state.persist()
            continue

        # one action per step keeps the trace clean and adaptation simple
        tc = resp.tool_calls[0]
        sig = f"{tc.name}:{json.dumps(tc.args, sort_keys=True)}"
        tracer.emit("tool_call", {"step": state.steps, "tool": tc.name, "args": tc.args})

        pre_findings = len(state.findings)
        pre_fetched = len(state.fetched_urls())
        try:
            obs = dispatch(tc.name, tc.args, state, tracer)
            ok = True
        except Exception as e:  # noqa: BLE001 - failures are data
            obs = f"ERROR: {e}"
            ok = False

        tracer.emit("observation", {"step": state.steps, "tool": tc.name, "ok": ok,
                                    "observation": obs[:1500]})
        state.push_turn(Turn(step=state.steps, tool=tc.name, args=tc.args, observation=obs[:800]))

        # ---- stall accounting -------------------------------------------
        progressed = (
            ok and (
                len(state.findings) > pre_findings
                or len(state.fetched_urls()) > pre_fetched
                or tc.name in ("update_plan", "finish")
            )
        )
        if sig == state.last_action_sig or not progressed:
            state.stall_counter += 1
        else:
            state.stall_counter = 0
        state.last_action_sig = sig

        # track search->fetch cadence: a fetch resets the run of searches
        if ok and tc.name == "web_search":
            state.searches_since_fetch += 1
        elif ok and tc.name == "fetch_url":
            state.searches_since_fetch = 0

        state.persist()

    # ---- fallback: never end a run without a grounded report ------------
    if not state.done:
        _force_synthesis(state, tracer)

    state.persist()
    tracer.emit("run_ended", {"done": state.done, "steps": state.steps,
                              "findings": len(state.findings),
                              "sources_fetched": len(state.fetched_urls()),
                              "nudges": state.nudges})
    return state


def _force_synthesis(state: ResearchState, tracer: Tracer) -> None:
    """Compose a final report directly from recorded findings when the budget
    runs out. Findings are grounded by construction (their URLs were fetched),
    so the resulting report is grounded too."""
    tracer.emit("force_synthesis", {"reason": "budget_exhausted",
                                     "findings": len(state.findings)})

    if not state.findings:
        state.final_report = (
            f"# {state.goal}\n\n"
            "The agent was unable to gather sufficient grounded evidence within its "
            "budget to answer this question. No verified findings were recorded."
        )
        state.done = True
        tracer.emit("finished", {"report": state.final_report, "sources_cited": [],
                                 "num_findings": 0})
        return

    findings_block = "\n".join(
        f"- {f.claim} | source: {f.source_url}" + (f' | quote: "{f.quote}"' if f.quote else "")
        for f in state.findings
    )
    user = f"GOAL: {state.goal}\n\nFINDINGS (use only these):\n{findings_block}"
    try:
        resp = llm.simple(SYNTHESIS, user, model=config.AGENT_MODEL)
        report = (resp.content or "").strip()
        state.tokens_used += resp.usage.get("total_tokens", 0)
    except Exception:
        # last-resort deterministic report so a run always produces something cited
        srcs = sorted({f.source_url for f in state.findings})
        body = "\n".join(f"- {f.claim} [{srcs.index(f.source_url) + 1}]" for f in state.findings)
        sources = "\n".join(f"[{i + 1}] {u}" for i, u in enumerate(srcs))
        report = f"# {state.goal}\n\n{body}\n\n## Sources\n{sources}"

    state.final_report = report
    state.done = True
    cited = sorted({f.source_url for f in state.findings})
    tracer.emit("finished", {"report": report, "sources_cited": cited,
                             "num_findings": len(state.findings)})

"""Central configuration. Everything tunable lives here, sourced from env with
sensible defaults so the agent runs out-of-the-box once API keys are set."""
from __future__ import annotations

import os
from pathlib import Path

try:
    from dotenv import load_dotenv

    load_dotenv()
except Exception:  # python-dotenv is optional; env may already be set
    pass


def _int(name: str, default: int) -> int:
    try:
        return int(os.environ.get(name, "").strip() or default)
    except ValueError:
        return default


# --- Paths ---------------------------------------------------------------
ROOT = Path(__file__).resolve().parent.parent
RUNS_DIR = ROOT / "runs"
CASSETTES_DIR = ROOT / "eval" / "cassettes"
RESULTS_DIR = ROOT / "eval" / "results"

# --- Models --------------------------------------------------------------
AGENT_MODEL = os.environ.get("AGENT_MODEL", "gpt-4o")
SUMMARY_MODEL = os.environ.get("SUMMARY_MODEL", "gpt-4o-mini")
JUDGE_MODEL = os.environ.get("JUDGE_MODEL", "gpt-4o-mini")

# --- Budgets (the levers that bound a long run) --------------------------
STEP_BUDGET = _int("STEP_BUDGET", 20)          # max agent iterations
TOKEN_BUDGET = _int("TOKEN_BUDGET", 120_000)   # cumulative prompt+completion tokens
MAX_SHORT_TERM = _int("MAX_SHORT_TERM", 4)     # raw tool turns kept verbatim in context
STALL_LIMIT = _int("STALL_LIMIT", 3)           # no-progress iterations before a nudge
SEARCH_WITHOUT_FETCH_LIMIT = _int("SEARCH_WITHOUT_FETCH_LIMIT", 3)  # searches in a row before a fetch nudge

# --- Tool tuning ---------------------------------------------------------
SEARCH_RESULTS = _int("SEARCH_RESULTS", 5)
PAGE_EXCERPT_CHARS = _int("PAGE_EXCERPT_CHARS", 2000)   # excerpt returned to the model
PAGE_MAX_CHARS = _int("PAGE_MAX_CHARS", 20_000)         # cap on stored page text
FETCH_TIMEOUT = _int("FETCH_TIMEOUT", 20)

# --- LLM cassette mode: live | record | replay --------------------------
LLM_MODE = os.environ.get("LLM_MODE", "live").strip().lower()

# --- Keys ----------------------------------------------------------------
OPENAI_API_KEY = os.environ.get("OPENAI_API_KEY", "")
TAVILY_API_KEY = os.environ.get("TAVILY_API_KEY", "")

115	    def pages_dir(self) -> Path:
116	        return self.run_dir / "pages"
117	
118	    @property
119	    def notes_dir(self) -> Path:
120	        return self.run_dir / "notes"
121	
122	    def write_note(self, name: str, content: str) -> None:
123	        """Store a scratchpad note in state and mirror it to disk for audit."""
124	        self.notes[name] = content
125	        self.notes_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
126	        safe = "".join(c if c.isalnum() or c in "-_." else "_" for c in name)[:80]
127	        (self.notes_dir / f"{safe}.txt").write_text(content, encoding="utf-8")
128	
129	    def persist(self) -> None:
130	        self.run_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
131	        tmp = self.run_dir / "state.json.tmp"
132	        tmp.write_text(json.dumps(self.to_dict(), indent=2), encoding="utf-8")
133	        tmp.replace(self.run_dir / "state.json")  # atomic-ish on same filesystem
134	
135	    # ---- helpers --------------------------------------------------------
136	    def add_source(self, url: str, title: str = "") -> Source:
137	        src = self.sources.get(url)
138	        if src is None:
139	            src = Source(url=url, title=title)
140	            self.sources[url] = src
141	        elif title and not src.title:
142	            src.title = title
143	        return src
144	
145	    def fetched_urls(self) -> set[str]:
146	        return {u for u, s in self.sources.items() if s.fetched}
147	
148	    def push_turn(self, turn: Turn) -> None:
149	        self.short_term.append(turn)
150	        if len(self.short_term) > config.MAX_SHORT_TERM:
151	            self.short_term = self.short_term[-config.MAX_SHORT_TERM:]
152	
153	    # ---- (de)serialisation ---------------------------------------------
154	    def to_dict(self) -> dict:
155	        return {
156	            "goal": self.goal,
157	            "run_id": self.run_id,
158	            "created_at": self.created_at,
159	            "steps": self.steps,
160	            "done": self.done,
161	            "final_report": self.final_report,
162	            "tokens_used": self.tokens_used,
163	            "plan": [asdict(p) for p in self.plan],
164	            "findings": [asdict(f) for f in self.findings],
165	            "sources": {u: asdict(s) for u, s in self.sources.items()},
166	            "short_term": [asdict(t) for t in self.short_term],
167	            "notes": self.notes,
168	            "stall_counter": self.stall_counter,
169	            "last_action_sig": self.last_action_sig,
170	            "nudges": self.nudges,
171	            "searches_since_fetch": self.searches_since_fetch,
172	            "fault": self.fault,
173	        }
174	
175	    @classmethod
176	    def from_dict(cls, d: dict) -> "ResearchState":
177	        st = cls(goal=d["goal"], run_id=d["run_id"])
178	        st.created_at = d.get("created_at", _now())
179	        st.steps = d.get("steps", 0)
180	        st.done = d.get("done", False)
181	        st.final_report = d.get("final_report", "")
182	        st.tokens_used = d.get("tokens_used", 0)
183	        st.plan = [PlanStep(**p) for p in d.get("plan", [])]
184	        st.findings = [Finding(**f) for f in d.get("findings", [])]
185	        st.sources = {u: Source(**s) for u, s in d.get("sources", {}).items()}
186	        st.short_term = [Turn(**t) for t in d.get("short_term", [])]
187	        st.notes = d.get("notes", {})
188	        st.stall_counter = d.get("stall_counter", 0)
189	        st.last_action_sig = d.get("last_action_sig", "")
190	        st.nudges = d.get("nudges", 0)
191	        st.searches_since_fetch = d.get("searches_since_fetch", 0)
192	        st.fault = d.get("fault", {})
193	        return st
194

120	        "model": model,
121	        "messages": messages,
122	        "tools": tools,
123	        "tool_choice": tool_choice,
124	        "temperature": temperature,
125	    }
126	    key = _cassette_key(payload)
127	
128	    # record + replay both read an existing cassette first. record is therefore
129	    # WRITE-ONCE (never clobbers), so repeated identical prompts in one run return
130	    # the same value — essential because the model isn't perfectly deterministic
131	    # even at temperature 0, and summary prompts can recur.
132	    if config.LLM_MODE in ("replay", "record"):
133	        cached = _load_cassette(key)
134	        if cached is not None:
135	            return _from_dict(cached)
136	        if config.LLM_MODE == "replay":
137	            raise RuntimeError(
138	                f"No cassette for request {key} (LLM_MODE=replay). "
139	                f"Record it first with LLM_MODE=record."
140	            )
141	
142	    kwargs = {"model": model, "messages": messages, "temperature": temperature}
143	    if tools:
144	        kwargs["tools"] = tools
145	        if tool_choice:
146	            kwargs["tool_choice"] = tool_choice
147	
148	    last_err = None
149	    for attempt in range(max_retries):
150	        try:
151	            raw = _get_client().chat.completions.create(**kwargs)
152	            resp = _normalise(raw)
153	            if config.LLM_MODE == "record":
154	                _save_cassette(key, _to_dict(resp))
155	            return resp
156	        except Exception as e:  # noqa: BLE001 - broad on purpose for transient retry
157	            last_err = e
158	            if not _is_transient(e) or attempt == max_retries - 1:
159	                raise
160	            time.sleep(min(2 ** attempt, 8))
161	    raise last_err  # pragma: no cover
162	
163	
164	def _is_transient(e: Exception) -> bool:
165	    name = type(e).__name__
166	    transient = {"RateLimitError", "APITimeoutError", "APIConnectionError", "InternalServerError"}
167	    if name in transient:
168	        return True
169	    status = getattr(e, "status_code", None)
170	    return status in (429, 500, 502, 503, 504)
171	
172	
173	def simple(system: str, user: str, model: Optional[str] = None, temperature: float = 0.0) -> LLMResponse:
174	    """Convenience for plain text completions (summaries, judging)."""
175	    return chat(
176	        messages=[{"role": "system", "content": system}, {"role": "user", "content": user}],
177	        model=model,
178	        temperature=temperature,
179	    )

layer for deterministic/offline replay.

Design choice: we do NOT use the multi-turn tool-message protocol. Each turn the
loop hands us a freshly *reconstructed* prompt (system + a rendered state
header) and we return the model's chosen tool call. This keeps every LLM call
effectively stateless and is what lets the agent run many steps without the
context growing unbounded (see context.py).

The cassette layer keys each request by a hash of its content. In `record` mode
we call the API and save the normalised response; in `replay` mode we serve it
from disk with no network. The eval harness uses replay for free, deterministic
reruns.

"""Eval runner + scorecard.

Runs the agent on each task (headless), then scores each finished run with the
deterministic checks and the LLM judge. Prints a scorecard and writes the full
results JSON to eval/results/. Under LLM_MODE=replay the whole suite runs
offline and deterministically from recorded cassettes.

Usage:
  python -m eval run                 # all tasks, with judge
  python -m eval run --task rust-vs-go
  python -m eval run --no-judge --max-steps 12
  python -m eval list
"""
from __future__ import annotations

import argparse
import json
import shutil
import sys
import time

from agent import config, loop
from agent.tracer import read_trace
from . import checks as checks_mod
from . import judge as judge_mod
from .tasks import TASKS, by_id


def run_task(task, max_steps=None, do_judge=True) -> dict:
    run_id = f"eval-{task.id}"
    # always start from a clean run dir so checks see only this attempt
    shutil.rmtree(config.RUNS_DIR / run_id, ignore_errors=True)

    fault = {task.fault: True} if task.fault else None
    t0 = time.time()
    state = loop.run(task.goal, run_id=run_id, fault=fault, max_steps=max_steps)
    elapsed = round(time.time() - t0, 1)
    trace = read_trace(run_id)

    check_results = checks_mod.run_checks(task, state, trace)
    passed = sum(1 for _, ok, _ in check_results if ok)

    result = {
        "id": task.id,
        "goal": task.goal,
        "fault": task.fault,
        "done": state.done,
        "steps": state.steps,
        "findings": len(state.findings),
        "sources_fetched": len(state.fetched_urls()),
        "nudges": state.nudges,
        "elapsed_s": elapsed,
        "checks": [{"name": n, "passed": ok, "detail": d} for n, ok, d in check_results],
        "checks_passed": passed,
        "checks_total": len(check_results),
        "judge": None,
    }
    if do_judge:
        result["judge"] = judge_mod.judge(task.goal, state.final_report, state.findings)
    return result


def _print_scorecard(results: list[dict]) -> None:
    print("\n" + "=" * 78)
    print(f"{'TASK':<22}{'DONE':<6}{'STEPS':<6}{'FIND':<6}{'CHECKS':<9}{'JUDGE avg':<10}")
    print("-" * 78)
    for r in results:
        j = r.get("judge") or {}
        javg = j.get("avg", "-")
        print(f"{r['id']:<22}{str(r['done']):<6}{r['steps']:<6}{r['findings']:<6}"
              f"{str(r['checks_passed'])+'/'+str(r['checks_total']):<9}{str(javg):<10}")
    print("-" * 78)
    total_p = sum(r["checks_passed"] for r in results)
    total_t = sum(r["checks_total"] for r in results)
    judged = [r["judge"]["avg"] for r in results if r.get("judge") and "avg" in r["judge"]]
    overall_judge = round(sum(judged) / len(judged), 2) if judged else "-"
    print(f"DETERMINISTIC CHECKS: {total_p}/{total_t} passed   |   "
          f"MEAN JUDGE SCORE: {overall_judge}")
    print("=" * 78)
    # surface any failing checks explicitly
    for r in results:
        fails = [c for c in r["checks"] if not c["passed"]]
        if fails:
            print(f"  ! {r['id']}: " + "; ".join(f"{c['name']} ({c['detail']})" for c in fails))


def main(argv=None) -> int:
    argv = argv if argv is not None else sys.argv[1:]
    p = argparse.ArgumentParser(prog="eval")
    sub = p.add_subparsers(dest="cmd", required=True)
    pr = sub.add_parser("run")
    pr.add_argument("--task", default=None, help="run a single task by id")
    pr.add_argument("--no-judge", action="store_true", help="skip the LLM judge")
    pr.add_argument("--max-steps", type=int, default=None)
    sub.add_parser("list")
    args = p.parse_args(argv)

    if args.cmd == "list":
        for t in TASKS:
            print(f"{t.id:<22} min_sources={t.min_sources} fault={t.fault or '-'}")
            print(f"    {t.goal}")
        return 0

    tasks = [by_id(args.task)] if args.task else TASKS
    if tasks == [None]:
        print(f"unknown task: {args.task}")
        return 2

    results = []
    for t in tasks:
        print(f"\n>>> running task: {t.id}")
        results.append(run_task(t, max_steps=args.max_steps, do_judge=not args.no_judge))

    _print_scorecard(results)

    config.RESULTS_DIR.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    out = config.RESULTS_DIR / (time.strftime("%Y%m%d-%H%M%S") + ".json")
    out.write_text(json.dumps({"mode": config.LLM_MODE, "results": results}, indent=2), encoding="utf-8")
    print(f"\nWrote scorecard to {out}")

    all_pass = all(r["checks_passed"] == r["checks_total"] for r in results)
    return 0 if all_pass else 1


if __name__ == "__main__":
    raise SystemExit(main())

"""The eval task set. A small, fixed suite of research goals chosen to exercise
different behaviours: multi-part synthesis, ambiguity that rewards re-planning,
and one fault-injection task that tests recovery from a failed fetch."""
from __future__ import annotations

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass
class Task:
    id: str
    goal: str
    min_sources: int
    rubric_notes: str
    fault: str | None = None  # e.g. "fail_first_fetch"


TASKS: list[Task] = [
    Task(
        id="carbon-capture",
        goal=("Compare the carbon-capture approaches of three companies: Climeworks, "
              "Charm Industrial, and Heirloom. For each, summarise the core method and "
              "one recent milestone."),
        min_sources=3,
        rubric_notes="Should distinguish the three distinct methods (DAC, bio-oil, mineralisation) and cite a source per company.",
    ),
    Task(
        id="llm-long-context",
        goal=("What context-window sizes do the latest GPT-4-class and Claude models "
              "support, and what are the practical trade-offs of using very long contexts?"),
        min_sources=3,
        rubric_notes="Should give concrete token numbers and discuss trade-offs (cost, latency, lost-in-the-middle).",
    ),
    Task(
        id="rust-vs-go",
        goal=("For building a high-concurrency network service, compare Rust and Go on "
              "performance, memory safety, and developer ergonomics."),
        min_sources=3,
        rubric_notes="Multi-dimensional comparison; rewards re-planning across the three axes.",
    ),
    Task(
        id="tavily-api",
        goal="What is the Tavily API, what are its main features, and how is it priced?",
        min_sources=2,
        rubric_notes="Recovery test: first fetch is forced to fail; agent must adapt and still finish cited.",
        fault="fail_first_fetch",
    ),
    Task(
        id="alzheimers-treatments",
        goal="What are the most promising treatments currently being researched for early-onset Alzheimer's disease?",
        min_sources=3,
        rubric_notes="Ambiguous/broad; rewards narrowing the plan to specific treatment classes.",
    ),
    Task(
        id="spacex-falcon1",
        goal=("Who founded SpaceX and in what year, and what were the key milestones of "
              "the Falcon 1 program?"),
        min_sources=2,
        rubric_notes="Multi-part factual; should answer founder, year, and Falcon 1 milestones.",
    ),
]


def by_id(task_id: str) -> Task | None:
    return next((t for t in TASKS if t.id == task_id), None)

file.content:
"""Deterministic, programmatic checks on a finished run. These need no LLM and
are fully reproducible — they measure *process* and *robustness*, complementing
the LLM judge which measures answer quality.

Each check returns (name, passed: bool, detail: str).
"""
from __future__ import annotations

import re

from agent import config

_URL_RE = re.compile(r"https?://[^\s\)\]\>\"']+")


def _cited_urls(report: str) -> set[str]:
    return {u.rstrip(".,);]") for u in _URL_RE.findall(report or "")}


def run_checks(task, state, trace) -> list[tuple[str, bool, str]]:
    results: list[tuple[str, bool, str]] = []
    fetched = state.fetched_urls()
    cited = _cited_urls(state.final_report)
    distinct_cited = cited & fetched

    results.append(("completed", state.done, f"done={state.done}"))
    results.append(("has_report", bool(state.final_report.strip()),
                    f"{len(state.final_report)} chars"))
    results.append((
        f"cited>={task.min_sources}",
        len(distinct_cited) >= task.min_sources,
        f"{len(distinct_cited)} distinct fetched sources cited",
    ))
    fabricated = cited - fetched
    results.append(("no_fabricated_citations", len(fabricated) == 0,
                    "none" if not fabricated else f"fabricated: {sorted(fabricated)}"))
    results.append(("within_step_budget", state.steps <= config.STEP_BUDGET,
                    f"{state.steps}/{config.STEP_BUDGET} steps"))

    # robustness: if a fault was injected, confirm the agent saw a failure and
    # still recovered to a completed, grounded report.
    if task.fault:
        had_error = any(e["type"] == "observation" and not e["data"].get("ok", True)
                        for e in trace)
        recovered = had_error and state.done and len(state.findings) >= 1
        results.append(("recovered_from_fault", recovered,
                        f"saw_error={had_error}, done={state.done}, findings={len(state.findings)}"))

    return results

content.content:
1	"""Bounded context construction — the core prompt/context-engineering piece.
2	
3	Instead of an ever-growing transcript, we rebuild a compact prompt from the
4	structured state every turn: goal, budget, plan, findings, a source index, and
5	only the last K raw tool turns. Older raw tool output is dropped because its
6	value was already distilled into findings and source summaries. This keeps the
7	prompt roughly constant in size no matter how long the run goes.
8	"""
9	from __future__ import annotations
10	
11	from . import config
12	from .prompts import SYSTEM
13	from .state import ResearchState
14	
15	_STATUS_MARK = {"done": "[x]", "active": "[>]", "dropped": "[-]", "pending": "[ ]"}
16	
17	
18	def _render_plan(state: ResearchState) -> str:
19	    if not state.plan:
20	        return "(no plan yet — call update_plan first)"
21	    return "\n".join(f"{_STATUS_MARK.get(p.status, '[ ]')} {p.id}. {p.text}" for p in state.plan)
22	
23	
24	def _source_index(state: ResearchState) -> dict[str, int]:
25	    """Stable numbering for fetched sources, for [n]-style citation guidance."""
26	    idx = {}
27	    n = 0
28	    for url, src in state.sources.items():
29	        if src.fetched:
30	            n += 1
31	            idx[url] = n
32	    return idx
33	
34	
35	def _render_sources(state: ResearchState, idx: dict[str, int]) -> str:
36	    lines = []
37	    for url, src in state.sources.items():
38	        if src.fetched:
39	            n = idx[url]
40	            lines.append(f"[{n}] {src.title or '(untitled)'} — {url}")
41	            if src.summary:
42	                lines.append(f"      summary: {src.summary}")
43	        else:
44	            lines.append(f"( - ) {src.title or '(untitled)'} — {url}  (not fetched)")
45	    return "\n".join(lines) if lines else "(none yet)"
46	
47	
48	def _render_findings(state: ResearchState, idx: dict[str, int]) -> str:
49	    if not state.findings:
50	        return "(none yet)"
51	    lines = []
52	    for i, f in enumerate(state.findings, 1):
53	        si = idx.get(f.source_url)
54	        tag = f"source [{si}]" if si else f.source_url
55	        quote = f' "{f.quote[:160]}"' if f.quote else ""
56	        lines.append(f"{i}. {f.claim} ({tag}){quote}")
57	    return "\n".join(lines)
58

content.subject: WI-4 retry jitter + WI-3 wall-clock budget
content.description: WI-4: jittered backoff in llm.py (random.uniform(0,min(2**attempt,8))). WI-3: WALL_CLOCK_BUDGET config + deadline in loop while-condition, emit budget_event{kind:wall_clock} and force_synthesis on breach; recompute on resume.

content.subject: WI-1 fresh-context critic/verifier
content.description: agent/verifier.py verify(state)->issues via one llm.simple call given only goal+findings ledger+draft (not worker reasoning). Deterministic fallback: flag finding with empty quote. Loop: after finish succeeds, run verify; if issues and verify_retries<MAX, set done=False, note=critique, emit verify_failed, continue; on exhaustion emit verify_exhausted. Also once in _force_synthesis. CRITIC prompt. Config VERIFY_ENABLED/_bool, MAX_VERIFY_RETRIES. State verify_retries round-trip. Tracer events. Eval check claims_verified.

content.subject: WI-2 real context compaction
content.description: Render most recent COMPACT_FINDINGS_RECENT findings verbatim; collapse older into state.findings_digest; same for sources_digest. Never drop from state (finish sees all). Stable [n] numbering. Compaction routine on cheap model every COMPACT_EVERY steps; deterministic fallback=truncate/concat. Config knobs. State digests round-trip. Tracer compaction event. Test: 40-finding state renders bounded+flat; finish sees all.

content.subject: WI-5 eval trajectory metrics + 4 failure scenarios
content.description: harness.py: derive tool_error_rate, replans, nudges-by-kind, verify_retries, compactions from trace; print compact, full in JSON. tasks.py + fetch/search injection toggles (recorded/mock, deterministic): empty/garbage results, contradictory sources, hallucinated citation (critic catches), kill+resume. Assert behaviors. New eval checks.

content.subject: WI-6 resume reorientation + WI-7 trace CLI
content.description: WI-6: on resume, one REORIENT prompt (llm.simple, deterministic fallback) before any tool call; emit reoriented. WI-7: `python -m agent trace <run_id> [--verbose]` renders trace.jsonl as step->action->observation->budget timeline. Document tail -f / watch in README.

content.subject: WI-8 kill switch + steering, WI-9 dedup polish
content.description: WI-8: before each tool exec check runs/<id>/AGENT_STOP (+SIGINT) -> persist, emit run_ended{status:aborted}, exit; check runs/<id>/STEER.md -> inject once as note, emit steer_injected, delete. WI-9: record_finding dedup on (claim,source_url); windowed loop-signature detector (ring buffer, REPEAT_LIMIT).

content.subject: Re-record eval, verify replay, README + CHANGELOG
content.description: Run offline tests; re-record eval cassettes live with new architecture; verify replay reproduces; regenerate example run; update README (failure-mode scorecard, new sections, results); write CHANGELOG.md documenting all WI changes; commit + push.

1	"""System and synthesis prompts. Kept in one place so prompt-engineering is
2	easy to find and tweak."""
3	
4	SYSTEM = """You are deepagent, a meticulous research assistant that works in a loop.
5	
6	Each turn you are shown the current STATE of your research (goal, plan, findings,
7	known sources, recent actions, remaining budget). You then choose EXACTLY ONE next
8	action by calling ONE tool. You do not write prose outside of tool calls.
9	
10	Your tools:
11	- update_plan: lay out or revise your plan of sub-questions. Call this first.
12	- web_search: find sources for a sub-question.
13	- fetch_url: read a source. You must fetch a source before you can cite it.
14	- record_finding: save a factual claim with the exact source URL and a short quote.
15	- write_note / read_note: a scratchpad for working memory (outlines, per-subtopic
16	  summaries, a draft of the comparison). Notes persist but stay out of your prompt
17	  until you read them — use them to think without burning context.
18	- finish: write the final markdown report when you have enough grounded findings.
19	
20	How to work well:
21	1. Start by calling update_plan with 3-6 concrete sub-questions.
22	2. Work ONE sub-question at a time. The cadence is: search -> fetch the most
23	   promising 1-2 results -> record findings from what you READ. Do not run several
24	   searches in a row without fetching — a search result snippet is not evidence.
25	3. Keep the plan current: mark the step you're on 'active' and finished steps
26	   'done' by calling update_plan again. Re-plan when a line dead-ends.
27	4. Use write_note to draft an outline or stash a per-subtopic summary so you can
28	   keep going without holding everything in context; read_note before you finish.
29	5. Avoid repeating the same search or fetching the same URL twice.
30	6. When your findings cover the goal (or budget is nearly spent), call finish.
31	
32	Notes vs findings: notes are scratch thinking; FINDINGS are the grounded evidence
33	that backs the report. Every claim in the report must trace to a record_finding
34	citing a fetched URL — never cite from a note or a search snippet alone.
35	
36	Grounding rules (strict):
37	- Only record findings and only cite URLs that you have actually fetched.
38	- In the final report, cite claims inline as [n] and end with a '## Sources'
39	  section listing each source as '[n] title — url'. Never invent sources or URLs.
40	
41	Be efficient: you have a limited step and token budget. Don't over-search; read,
42	record, and synthesise."""
43	
44	
45	SYNTHESIS = """You are writing the FINAL research report because the agent's budget is
46	exhausted. Use ONLY the findings provided (each has a source URL). Write a clear,
47	well-structured markdown report that answers the goal as fully as the evidence
48	allows. Cite claims inline as [n] and end with a '## Sources' section listing each
49	source as '[n] title — url'. Do not invent facts or sources beyond those given.
50	If the evidence is thin, say so honestly."""
51

"""System and synthesis prompts. Kept in one place so prompt-engineering is
easy to find and tweak."""

SYSTEM = """You are deepagent, a meticulous research assistant that works in a loop.

Each turn you are shown the current STATE of your research (goal, plan, findings,
known sources, recent actions, remaining budget). You then choose EXACTLY ONE next
action by calling ONE tool. You do not write prose outside of tool calls.

Your tools:
- update_plan: lay out or revise your plan of sub-questions. Call this first.
- web_search: find sources for a sub-question.
- fetch_url: read a source. You must fetch a source before you can cite it.
- record_finding: save a factual claim with the exact source URL and a short quote.
- write_note / read_note: a scratchpad for working memory (outlines, per-subtopic
  summaries, a draft of the comparison). Notes persist but stay out of your prompt
  until you read them — use them to think without burning context.
- finish: write the final markdown report when you have enough grounded findings.

How to work well:
1. Start by calling update_plan with 3-6 concrete sub-questions.
2. Work ONE sub-question at a time. The cadence is: search -> fetch the most
   promising 1-2 results -> record findings from what you READ. Do not run several
   searches in a row without fetching — a search result snippet is not evidence.
3. Keep the plan current: mark the step you're on 'active' and finished steps
   'done' by calling update_plan again. Re-plan when a line dead-ends.
4. Use write_note to draft an outline or stash a per-subtopic summary so you can
   keep going without holding everything in context; read_note before you finish.
5. Avoid repeating the same search or fetching the same URL twice.
6. When your findings cover the goal (or budget is nearly spent), call finish.

Notes vs findings: notes are scratch thinking; FINDINGS are the grounded evidence
that backs the report. Every claim in the report must trace to a record_finding
citing a fetched URL — never cite from a note or a search snippet alone.

Grounding rules (strict):
- Only record findings and only cite URLs that you have actually fetched.
- In the final report, cite claims inline as [n] and end with a '## Sources'
  section listing each source as '[n] title — url'. Never invent sources or URLs.

Be efficient: you have a limited step and token budget. Don't over-search; read,
record, and synthesise."""


SYNTHESIS = """You are writing the FINAL research report because the agent's budget is
exhausted. Use ONLY the findings provided (each has a source URL). Write a clear,
well-structured markdown report that answers the goal as fully as the evidence
allows. Cite claims inline as [n] and end with a '## Sources' section listing each
source as '[n] title — url'. Do not invent facts or sources beyond those given.
If the evidence is thin, say so honestly."""

"""Headless CLI: `python -m agent run "<goal>"` (also used by the eval harness)
and `python -m agent resume <run_id>`."""
from __future__ import annotations

import argparse
import sys

from . import config
from .loop import run


def _print_summary(state) -> None:
    print("\n" + "=" * 70)
    print(f"RUN {state.run_id}  |  done={state.done}  steps={state.steps}  "
          f"findings={len(state.findings)}  sources={len(state.fetched_urls())}")
    print("=" * 70)
    print(state.final_report or "(no report)")
    print("-" * 70)
    print(f"Artifacts: {state.run_dir}")
    print(f"  trace : {state.run_dir / 'trace.jsonl'}")
    print(f"  state : {state.run_dir / 'state.json'}")


def main(argv=None) -> int:
    argv = argv if argv is not None else sys.argv[1:]
    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(prog="agent", description="deepagent research assistant")
    sub = parser.add_subparsers(dest="cmd", required=True)

    p_run = sub.add_parser("run", help="run a new research task")
    p_run.add_argument("goal", help="the research question / goal")
    p_run.add_argument("--run-id", default=None, help="fixed run id (optional)")
    p_run.add_argument("--max-steps", type=int, default=None, help="override step budget")
    p_run.add_argument("--fault", default=None,
                       help="inject a fault, e.g. 'fail_first_fetch' (for eval/testing)")

    p_res = sub.add_parser("resume", help="resume an interrupted run")
    p_res.add_argument("run_id", help="the run id to resume")
    p_res.add_argument("--max-steps", type=int, default=None)

    args = parser.parse_args(argv)

    if args.cmd == "run":
        fault = {args.fault: True} if args.fault else None
        state = run(args.goal, run_id=args.run_id, fault=fault, max_steps=args.max_steps)
    else:  # resume
        state = run(goal="", run_id=args.run_id, resume=True, max_steps=args.max_steps)

    _print_summary(state)
    return 0 if state.done else 1


if __name__ == "__main__":
    raise SystemExit(main())

def _download_and_extract(url: str):
    """Download + extract readable text, cached for offline replay.
    Returns [title, text]."""
    def _produce():
        try:
            resp = requests.get(url, headers=_HEADERS, timeout=config.FETCH_TIMEOUT)
            resp.raise_for_status()
        except Exception as e:
            raise RuntimeError(f"failed to fetch {url}: {e}")
        title, text = _extract(resp.text)
        if not text:
            raise RuntimeError(f"could not extract readable text from {url}")
        return [title, text[: config.PAGE_MAX_CHARS]]

    return cached("fetch", url, _produce)


def fetch_url(args: dict, state, tracer) -> str:
    url = (args.get("url") or "").strip()
    if not url:
        raise RuntimeError("fetch_url requires a 'url'")

    # eval fault-injection: force the first fetch to fail to test recovery
    if state.fault.get("fail_first_fetch") and not state.fault.get("_fetch_failed_once"):
        state.fault["_fetch_failed_once"] = True
        raise RuntimeError(f"network error fetching {url} (injected fault)")

    title, text = _download_and_extract(url)

    # store full text on disk, out of context
    h = hashlib.sha256(url.encode()).hexdigest()[:12]
    page_file = f"pages/{h}.txt"
    (state.pages_dir / f"{h}.txt").write_text(text, encoding="utf-8")

    summary = _summarise(state.goal, url, text)

    src = state.add_source(url, title)
    src.fetched = True
    src.summary = summary
    src.page_file = page_file

    excerpt = text[: config.PAGE_EXCERPT_CHARS]
    return (
